name: giil
description: Get Image [from] Internet Link - Zero-setup CLI for downloading full-resolution images from iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos, and Google Drive share links. Four-tier capture strategy, browser automation, HEIC conversion, album support. Node.js/Playwright.
capabilities: Downloads images from iCloud share links using browser automation
Extracts full-resolution images from Google Photos and Drive
Converts HEIC/HEIF images to standard formats automatically
Downloads entire photo albums with indexed filenames
Provides JSON output for AI tool integration
Uses four-tier capture strategy for maximum reliability
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation for the giil CLI tool. The static scanner flagged 226 patterns in documentation, but all are false positives. Patterns like 'pipe to shell' and 'command substitution' are standard shell scripting examples for software installation and usage, not malicious code. The skill is pure documentation with no executable components.
valueStatement: Stop struggling to get screenshots to your remote AI sessions. giil downloads full-resolution images from iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos, and Drive share links with one simple command.
userTitle: Download Images from Cloud Share Links
limitations: Requires internet connection to cloud services
First run downloads about 200MB Chromium browser
Some links require public sharing enabled by owner
Linux users need to install HEIC conversion libraries manually

useCases.title: Debug UI issues remotely
useCases.description: SSH into servers and download iPhone screenshots directly to your remote AI coding session

useCases.title: Share visual context instantly
useCases.description: Paste cloud photo links to Claude Code for immediate visual analysis without file transfers

useCases.title: Batch download photo albums
useCases.description: Download entire shared albums from family events or work projects with organized filenames

promptTemplates.title: Basic image download
promptTemplates.prompt: Use giil to download this image: https://share.icloud.com/photos/xxx

promptTemplates.title: JSON output mode
promptTemplates.prompt: Download this image with JSON metadata: giil "URL" --json

promptTemplates.title: Album download
promptTemplates.prompt: Download all photos from this album to ~/Downloads: giil "URL" --all --output ~/Downloads

promptTemplates.title: Debug mode
promptTemplates.prompt: Debug why this image will not download: giil "URL" --debug --verbose

bestPractices: Use --json flag for reliable parsing in scripts and AI workflows
Enable --debug mode when troubleshooting failed downloads
Install HEIC support on Linux before processing Apple photos
antiPatterns: Do not use giil for private links without public sharing enabled
Avoid downloading copyrighted content you do not have rights to
Do not rely on viewport screenshots for quality-critical work

faq.question: Is giil compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, giil outputs JSON that Claude Code can parse for image analysis workflows.

faq.question: What are the download limits?
faq.answer: No hard limits exist, but cloud services may rate-limit bulk downloads. Use polite delays.

faq.question: Can I integrate giil with my scripts?
faq.answer: Yes, use --json flag for structured output and --output to specify directories.

faq.question: Is my data safe with giil?
faq.answer: Processing happens locally. No data is sent anywhere except to cloud services you specify.

faq.question: Why am I getting Auth required errors?
faq.answer: The link is not publicly shared. Ask the owner to enable public access in their cloud settings.

faq.question: How does giil compare to wget or curl?
faq.answer: giil handles JavaScript-heavy cloud services that wget and curl cannot, with automatic quality optimization.
